qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of this document?
qa_pairs.answer: This document provides instructions on how to set up and access Arbutus object storage using WinSCP.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one download WinSCP?
qa_pairs.answer: WinSCP can be downloaded from https://winscp.net/.

qa_pairs.question: What are the required basic configuration settings for a new WinSCP session to connect to Arbutus object storage?
qa_pairs.answer: The required settings include File protocol (Amazon S3), Host name (object-arbutus.alliancecan.ca), Port number (443), and your 20-digit Access key ID.

qa_pairs.question: Which advanced setting in WinSCP is crucial for connecting to Arbutus object storage and must be changed from its default?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'URL style:' setting, located under 'Advanced...' -> 'Environment' -> 'S3' -> 'Protocol options', must be changed from 'Virtual Host' to 'Path'.

qa_pairs.question: What issue will occur if the 'URL style' setting is not configured to 'Path'?
qa_pairs.answer: If the 'URL style' setting is not changed to 'Path', WinSCP will not work and will display hostname resolution errors.

qa_pairs.question: After logging in, what common tasks can users perform using the WinSCP GUI?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can create buckets and transfer files using the WinSCP GUI after logging in.

qa_pairs.question: How can users set Access Control Lists (ACLs) for files in WinSCP?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can set a file's ACL by right-clicking on the file in WinSCP.
